pi_items.source: OrangeHRM 5.8 source code (src/plugins/orangehrmLeavePlugin/Controller/WorkWeekController.php) only offers 'Half Day' (id=4) for Work Week. Values 1 and 2 are for Leave Durations, not Work Week.
OrangeHRM 5.8 source code (src/plugins/orangehrmLeavePlugin/Controller/WorkWeekController.php) only offers 'Half Day' (id=4) for Work Week. Values 1 and 2 are for Leave Durations, not Work Week.
OrangeHRM 5.8 source code (src/plugins/orangehrmLeavePlugin/entity/WorkWeek.php) WORKWEEK_LENGTH_NON_WORKING_DAY = 8. The value 4 corresponds to 'Half Day'.
OrangeHRM 5.8 UI and WorkWeekController.php only provide 'Full Day', 'Half Day', and 'Non-working Day' options for Work Week configuration.

privileged_info_summary: The task metadata and verifier rely on incorrect Work Week status codes for OrangeHRM 5.8. Source code analysis reveals that the valid Work Week lengths in `ohrm_work_week` are 0 (Full Day), 4 (Half Day), and 8 (Non-working Day). The UI does not support distinguishing between 'Half Day - Morning' and 'Half Day - Afternoon'; it only offers 'Half Day'. The task's assumption that 4 represents 'Non-working' is false (4 is 'Half Day'), and its expectation that an agent can set a day to 1 ('Half Day - Morning') is impossible via the Work Week configuration UI.
